title: Assemblers (electrical and electronic products)
description: Assemble, fit, fasten, and install parts of airplanes, space vehicles, or missiles, such as tails, wings, fuselage, bulkheads, stabilizers, landing gear, rigging and control equipment, or heating and ventilating systems. Wind wire coils used in electrical components, such as resistors and transformers, and in electrical equipment and instruments, such as field cores, bobbins, armature cores, electrical motors, generators, and control equipment. Assemble or modify electrical or electronic equipment, such as computers, test equipment telemetering systems, electric motors, and batteries. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Assemble parts, fittings, or subassemblies on aircraft, using layout tools, hand tools, power tools, or fasteners, such as bolts, screws, rivets, or clamps.
Assemble electrical or electronic systems or support structures and install components, units, subassemblies, wiring, or assembly casings, using rivets, bolts, soldering, or micro-welding equipment.
Inspect, test, and adjust completed units to ensure that units meet specifications, tolerances, and customer order requirements.
Clean, lubricate, maintain, and adjust equipment to maintain efficient operation, using air hoses, cleaning fluids, and hand tools.
Transfer components, metal products, or assemblies, using moving equipment.
Read blueprints, work orders, or production schedules to determine product or job instructions or specifications.
Record production and operational data on specified forms.
Disassemble units to replace parts or to crate them for shipping.
Connect cables, tubes, and wiring, according to specifications.
Repair products by dismantling, straightening, reshaping, and reassembling parts, using cutting torches, straightening presses, and hand tools.
Examine workpieces for defects and measure workpieces with straightedges or templates to ensure conformance with specifications.
Inspect, operate, and test completed products to verify functioning, machine capabilities, or conformance to customer specifications.
Select torch tips, alloys, flux, coil, tubing, or wire, according to metal types or thicknesses, data charts, or records.
Assemble prefabricated parts to form subassemblies and fabricate parts needed for assembly or installation, using shop machinery or equipment.
Lay out, fit, or connect parts to be bonded, calculating production measurements, as necessary.
Position, align, or adjust workpieces or electrical parts to facilitate wiring or assembly.
Clean or degrease parts, using wire brushes, portable grinders, or chemical baths.
Adjust, repair, or replace electrical or electronic components to correct defects and to ensure conformance to specifications.
Devise or build fixtures or jigs used to hold parts in place during welding, brazing, or soldering.
Hammer out bulges or bends in metal workpieces and wear corrective items on manufactured metal parts, ensuring proper state for effective assembly.
Monitor the fitting, burning, and welding processes to avoid overheating of parts or warping, shrinking, distortion, or expansion of materials.
Measure parts to determine tolerances, using precision measuring instruments such as micrometers, calipers, and verniers.
Remove completed workpieces or parts from machinery, using hand tools, and remove excess weld, slag, or spatter to ensure clean transitions.
Align, fit,assemble, connect, or install system components, using jigs, fixtures, measuring instruments, hand tools, or power tools.
Read and interpret schematic drawings, diagrams, blueprints, specifications, work orders, or reports to determine materials requirements or assembly instructions.
Observe meters, gauges, or machine operations to ensure that soldering or brazing processes meet specifications and control functions are stable during operations.
Select and load materials such as workpieces, objects, and machine parts onto equipment used in coiling processes.
Conduct trial runs before welding, soldering, or brazing, and make necessary adjustments to equipment to maintain efficiency in assembly operations.
Monitor production output and assist in quality checks to ensure efficiency in assembly processes and meet customer expectations.
Examine components for defects, using visual inspection techniques, tools like loupes, and precise measuring instruments to ensure that assemblies are fit for purpose.
Fasten or install piping, fixtures, or wiring and electrical components to form assemblies or subassemblies, using hand tools, rivet guns, or welding equipment.
Train new employees on assembly procedures and techniques, ensuring compliance with safety protocols and quality standards.
Inspect or test installed units, parts, systems, or assemblies for fit, alignment, performance, defects, or compliance with standards, using measuring instruments or test equipment.
Lay out and drill, ream, tap, or cut parts for assembly and ensure that all parts are ready for effective joining processes.
Clean and lubricate timepiece parts and assemblies, using solvents, buff sticks, and oil as necessary during assembly operations.
Analyze engineering drawings, specifications, sketches, and work orders to plan layout, assembly, and operations effectively, ensuring comprehension of project requirements.
Assess and streamline assembly operations to promote efficiency, including positions and assembly line setups, while consistently meeting production targets.
Assemble and install components, modify and inspect electrical or electronic equipment, such as motors and test equipment, ensuring conformance to specifications and performing quality checks.
Observe operation of timepiece parts and subassemblies to determine accuracy of movement and diagnose causes of defects.
Examine and test wired electrical components, using measuring devices to record test results, analyze data, and ensure compliance with standards.
Supervise assemblers and train employees on job procedures while reviewing work orders to ensure work follows specifications.
Perform maintenance and minor repairs on production equipment and machinery, ensuring safety and proper functioning.
Monitor production operations or equipment, making necessary adjustments to ensure conformance to specifications and addressing production problems.
Record production data, including inspection or test data, and communicate production levels to management.
Clean and maintain work areas, machinery, and equipment to ensure safe working conditions and proper functioning.
Transfer, load, and unload materials and products between production areas, warehouses, and storage areas, using hand tools and powered equipment.
Measure dimensions of products and components to verify conformance to specifications, using appropriate measuring instruments.
Prepare finished products for shipment by packaging and sorting them.
Collect or select samples for testing or quality control, and record data related to production processes.
Operate machinery used in the assembly and production processes, assisting where necessary.
Lift and handle materials, finished products, and tools, ensuring safe practices.
Inspect products to ensure they conform to quality standards and specifications, rejecting defective items where necessary.
Read blueprints and production manuals to determine assembly specifications and procedures.
Write inspection or test reports detailing results and recommendations for repairs.
Signal coworkers and assist in loading and moving products during the production process.
Determine work assignments and manage team workflow based on production needs.
Store and maintain tools and materials to ensure organized work environments.
Collaborate with supervisors to troubleshoot machine malfunctions and production errors.
Use hand tools to fasten, package, or stack materials and products to ensure proper handling and shipping.
Participate in cleaning and inspecting collection equipment for routine maintenance and repairs.
Listen to machines during operation to detect sounds such as those made by dull cutting tools or excessive vibration, and adjust machines to compensate for problems.
Lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.
Stop machines to remove finished workpieces or to change tooling, setup, or workpiece placement, according to required machining sequences.
Adjust machine feed and speed, change cutting tools, or adjust machine controls when automatic programming is faulty or if machines malfunction.
Mount, install, align, and secure tools, attachments, fixtures, and workpieces on machines, using hand tools and precision measuring instruments.
Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments, templates, and fixtures.
Remove and replace dull cutting tools.
Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform one or more machine functions on metal or plastic workpieces.
Sort shop orders into groups to maximize materials utilization and minimize machine setup time.
Review program specifications or blueprints to determine and set machine operations and sequencing, finished workpiece dimensions, or numerical control sequences.
Monitor machine operation and control panel displays, and compare readings to specifications to detect malfunctions.
Write instruction sheets and cutter lists for a machine's controller to guide setup and encode numerical control tapes.
Prepare geometric layouts from graphic displays, using computer-assisted drafting software or drafting instruments and graph paper.
Enter computer commands to store or retrieve parts patterns, graphic displays, or programs that transfer data to other media.
Modify existing programs to enhance efficiency.
Write programs in the language of a machine's controller and store programs on media, such as punch tapes, magnetic tapes, or disks.
Compare encoded tapes or computer printouts with original part specifications and blueprints to verify accuracy of instructions.
Observe machines on trial runs or conduct computer simulations to ensure that programs and machinery will function properly and produce items that meet specifications.
Determine reference points, machine cutting paths, or hole locations, and compute angular and linear dimensions, radii, and curvatures.
Analyze job orders, drawings, blueprints, specifications, printed circuit board pattern films, and design data to calculate dimensions, tool selection, machine speeds, and feed rates.
Revise programs or tapes to eliminate errors, and retest programs to check that problems have been solved.
Determine the sequence of machine operations, and select the proper cutting tools needed to machine workpieces into the desired shapes.
Write simple programs for computer-controlled machine tools.
Implement changes to machine programs, and enter new specifications, using computers.
Clean machines, tooling, or parts, using solvents or solutions and rags.
Stack or load finished items, or place items on conveyor systems.
Maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, using hand tools.
Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines.
Order tooling for jobs.
